description: A borrowed basket finds its way back to the neighbour who lent it. Its coming back is 'turema'. The prior relation may be with a place or person, or it may be a condition or use. 'turema' follows the way back; 'kamo' (arrive) says it reached the endpoint.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't', release it, and raise the back of the tongue while rounding the lips for high-back 'u'. Make a tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge and move into stressed middle-height front 'e'. Close the lips for voiced 'm', carry the resonance through the nose, and finish with open central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: The dental release leads to a tap or short trill near the centre, then final 'ma' closes the lips before opening again. The contour bends and opens a second time, an echo of a path back towards familiar ground.
usage_notes: The returning person or thing is the subject. A spatial goal can take 'kau' (ALL), while 'wea' (TOWARD) keeps the motion only towards the earlier place. Causative 'ka' returns another person or thing.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: During meditation, attention wanders and is brought back to breath or bodily sensation. Causative 'ka' with 'turema' can name that small correction without treating the wandering as failure.

semantic_domains.activity: coming or going back towards an earlier place, state, relation, or use
semantic_domains.spatial: movement back towards a previous location
semantic_domains.temporal: an earlier relation providing the reference for what comes back later
